concept: Creative / Bringing New Things Into Being / Inventive
description: Relating to or involving the imagination and original ideas; able to create new things. 'Phisoe' names the quality of generative imagination—the capacity to bring forth what did not exist before.
sound_symbolism: The word shapes the new. 'Phi' /ɸi/ begins with forming breath, creation stirring. Then 'so' shapes with intention, form emerging. Finally 'e' opens to the new, creation complete.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'phisoe miona' (creative person). Central to artistic and innovation vocabulary. Related to 'wiero' (imaginative) but emphasizes actual creation rather than mental vision.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Creative solutions address seemingly impossible challenges—imagination applied to building sustainable futures.
pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Creativity was Art Nouveau's driving force—artists who saw new forms in nature and brought them into human-made objects.
